role_title: Product Manager of AI Applications, Global Public Sector

level_signals: Leads cross-functional team of engineers, MLEs, and operators
Owns large AI projects for one or many customers
Entrepreneurial and experienced product leader framing

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 4+ years of experience building products with specific experience within the last 1-2 years building AI-powered products
must_haves.qualifier: with recent 1-2 years specifically in AI-powered products

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Strong technical background (STEM degree) and/or experience building technical software products
Strong understanding of generative AI technologies and their applications in both enterprise and consumer settings
Experience with vibe coding tools (i.e., Replit, Lovable, Bolt, etc.) and design tools (i.e., Figma/Canva/Miro)
Exceptional leadership, presentation and communication skills with the ability to influence cross-functional teams

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Proficiency in Arabic, both written and spoken

explicitly_excluded: Candidates who have applied for the same role within the last 90 days are not reconsidered

scope.surface: Global Public Sector AI applications — custom AI solutions and custom LLMs for international governments and government-backed entities
scope.business_model: B2G (business-to-government), bespoke forward-deployed AI solutions, multi-million dollar contracts
primary_metrics_inferred: Revenue generated per customer engagement (millions in revenue)
Customer satisfaction and adoption of AI applications
core_responsibilities: Lead design workshops with the client to define custom AI solutions
Scope out new AI application use cases across various government entities
Lead cross-functional development of AI applications and custom LLMs with diverse stakeholders (Engineering + Ops + Go-to-Market)
Consistently engage with future end-users to solicit feedback and ensure we are prioritizing effectively
Stay up to date with latest research in applied AI and training custom LLMs
Scope out model evaluation sets and performance requirements, consistently review results, and iterate on the solution
Give regular progress updates to the client and Global Public Sector leadership

green_flags: Clear ownership scope: large AI projects for one or many public sector customers
Explicitly calls out international/global focus — differentiated from typical US-centric roles
Arabic language as a nice-to-have signals genuine regional deployment intent (likely MENA)
Company lists real government clients (Government of Qatar, U.S. Army, U.S. Air Force) — credibility signal
Role sits on a 'quickly expanding team' with direct revenue impact framing
red_flags: No location or city specified — remote vs. onsite vs. hybrid entirely unclear
No compensation range disclosed despite referencing US Pay Transparency compliance
Vague seniority framing ('entrepreneurial and experienced') without title level clarity
High buzzword density may obscure actual day-to-day scope

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years required, leadership framing, and cross-functional ownership)
scope.business_model (inferred from 'government and government-backed entities' and 'bespoke solutions')
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (not stated; inferred from responsibilities and 'millions in revenue' mention)
geographies_named on individual requirements (MENA inferred from Arabic nice-to-have but not stated explicitly as target geography)
ambiguities: JD does not specify a work location (city, country, or remote policy) — location.type set to 'unclear'
Compensation section references US Pay Transparency compliance but discloses no range — unclear whether role is US-based or global
Arabic language proficiency as a nice-to-have strongly suggests MENA regional focus, but the JD only says 'outside of the United States' broadly

Vibe coding tools requirement ('Replit, Lovable, Bolt') classified as hard/must-have per its placement under Minimum Qualifications, though it reads more like a tool-familiarity check than a gating criterion
